pull_requests.title: Suggestions for PR #589
pull_requests.body:
Review suggestions for #589. 

- Lint & formatting cleanup across changed files
  - consistent indentation, trailing commas, whitespace, docstrings moved to PEP 257 style
  - Modern type annotations: replace `Dict`, `List`, `Tuple`, `Optional` with `dict`, `list`, `tuple` builtins; add `Iterator` from `collections.abc`
  - Stricter iteration: add `strict=True/False` to `zip()` calls; replace bare `assert` with `ValueError` for runtime checks
  - Remove unused  `from spikeinterface import s

pull_requests.title: Improve SVG rendering in light and dark mode
pull_requests.body:
Closes #119 and #208 

- SVGs with line art (transparent/white background, black/coloured lines) rendered with visible white boxes in both light and dark mode.
  - In light mode: mix-blend-mode: multiply removes the baked-in white background so SVGs blend into the surrounding div.
  - In dark mode: filter: invert(1) hue-rotate(180deg) turns black lines white; mix-blend-mode: screen dissolves the inverted black background. A background fix on .sd-card-hover:hover prevents the blend from break

pull_requests.title: Redirect DataJoint attach downloads to tmp dir in tests and restrict populate calls to experiment being tested
pull_requests.body:
When running integration tests for OnixImuChunk, two related issues cause .joblib files to accumulate in cwd:
- OnixImuChunk.make() calls fetch1("sync_model"), which extracts .joblib files to dj.config["download_path"] (default is cwd) and these are never cleaned up after running the test suite
- All populate() calls are unrestricted. Since MySQL container is session-scoped, EphysSyncModel rows inserted by one test are visible to populate() calls in other tests, causing make() to process rows

pull_requests.title: Support PEP 695 `type` statement and python 3.12+ `TypeAliasType`
pull_requests.body:
Closes #690 

This PR
- adds an `if isinstance(annotation, TypeAliasType):` branch in `format_annotation` to handle [typing.TypeAliasType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.TypeAliasType) introduced in python 3.12
  - the logic is similar to how `TypeAliasForwardRef` is currently handled, extending that same lookup to all intermediate prefix levels, so aliases documented anywhere in the module hierarchy resolve correctly
- adds, for external TypeAliasType (defined in a di

pull_requests.title: Combine lint and test workflows
pull_requests.body:
This PR closes #555 
- combines lint and test in a single lint_and_test.yml
- configures tests to run on
  - ubuntu (all supported python versions, unit + integration tests)
  - macOs and windows (latest supported python version - 3.12, unit tests only as only the ubuntu runner has docker pre-installed)
- removes other unused workflows
- updates docker-aeon-mecha.yml to run on `main` (is this workflow still needed?)
 
Ruff is failing but this can be addressed in a separate PR.

pull_requests.title: Add workflow to squash-merge `datajoint_pipeline` PRs to `main`
pull_requests.body:
This PR replaces the squash-merge-to-prod workflow with one that squash-merges all merged PRs to datajoint_pipeline to `main`. 
The current workflow is to keep the full commit history on `datajoint_pipeline` and a summarised view on `main`, essentially replacing the previous `main`/`prod` with `datajoint_pipeline`/`main`.

pull_requests.title: Preserve images with 0 instances as empty labeled frames in DLC and COCO readers
pull_requests.body:
Closes #417 

This PR
- fixes the COCO reader to create a LabeledFrame for every image in the "images" array, regardless of whether it appears in "annotations"
- fixes the DLC reader to create a LabeledFrame for every CSV row, regardless of whether all coordinates are NaN
- adds tests/data/coco/flat_images/annotations_negative_frame.json, a COCO annotations file with 3 images and 2 annotations (image id 2 has no corresponding annotation to represent a negative frame)
- extends COCO read_la

pull_requests.title: Fetch tags upon checkout in docs build workflow
pull_requests.body: The actions/checkout step does not specify fetch-depth, so it defaults to 1 (a shallow clone with only the latest commit but no tags). In conf.py `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` then fails to find any tag and returns "".

pull_requests.title: Add concurrency settings to docs build workflow
pull_requests.body: This PR configures the docs build workflow such that any in-progress runs are cancelled if a new run is triggered for the same ref, except for tags and main branch pushes.

pull_requests.title: Disable uv caching for publish job in CI workflow
pull_requests.body:
Because the publish step only uses uv for publishing from built files (and not producing any uv-cacheable content, e.g. no dependencies were downloaded by uv), there isn't a cache which setup-uv expects to find at cleanup time.

The alternative would be to keep `enable-cache` as true (default) and set `ignore-nothing-to-cache: true` 

Closes #73

This PR 
- updates and adds missing class attribute docstrings in adherence to [PEP257 docstring conventions](https://peps.python.org/pep-0257/) for "attribute docstrings". This allows us to use type annotations in the docs. See also [sphinx autodoc's guide](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/extensions/autodoc.html#doc-comments-and-docstrings) on documenti

pull_requests.title: Remove 0-padding from example filenames
pull_requests.body:
In PR #26 I am adding a helper function to auto-generate filenames. When generating the file_name, I also want to ensure the frameIDs have consistent width. To do so, I am just inferring the max length to pad based on `max(all_frame_ids)`. Given the sample data has only frames 1000-1004, the auto-generated filenames should also only have length 4.

This PR
- removes 0-padding from the sample structure
- updates the poseinterface data spec 
  - to include examples where 0-padding is needed

Tests are failing because of #345.

pull_requests.title: Update sitemap exclusions to use directory paths
Improve `make` files
pull_requests.body:
- standardises `make.bat` to support `make clean html` rather than needing `.\make clean && .\make html`) (Makefile already supports this)
- removes copied example notebooks in src dir in `clean` command
- removes CNAME file automatically generated by [sphinx.ext.githubpages](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/extensions/githubpages.html#module-sphinx.ext.githubpages) as this causes GitHub to automatically configure custom domain name (aeon.swc.ucl.ac.uk, a reverse proxy to the GH page

pull_requests.title: Configure redirects for moved pages mentioned in Aeon platform paper

pull_requests.title: Use Sphinx `dirhtml` builder
pull_requests.body:
See #217 

The aeon homepage is not indexed because of the following error:
Duplicate, Google chose different canonical than user
User-declared canonical: https://aeon.swc.ucl.ac.uk/index.html
Google-selected canonical: https://aeon.swc.ucl.ac.uk/

This is because the sitemap.xml generated using sphinx-sitemap contains https://aeon.swc.ucl.ac.uk/index.html instead of the dir URL. To work around this, we switch to the `dirhtml` builder instead of the default `html` builder.

pull_requests.title: Create sample Train directory structure
pull_requests.body:
Closes #18 

Added in Clips
- a clip with 5 frames (.mp4)
- the corresponding cliplabels.json (image_ids have been changed to 0-based)

Added in Frames
- the same 5 frames extracted from the clip (.png)
- the corresponding framelabels.json** (image_id is the video frame index) 

** here we have consecutive frame indices, but we would typically have random frames

## TODO
- [x] use frame files as clip file_name
- [x] remove extension from clip file_name because these frame files may

pull_requests.title: Use explicit targets to refer to Aeon docs pages and update redirected URLs
Add sitemap generation support and robots.txt file
pull_requests.body: Helps resolve some indexing confusion (#217)

pull_requests.title: Remove duplicated check-manifest step
pull_requests.body:
## Description

**What is this PR**

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Addition of a new feature
- [ ] Other

**Why is this PR needed?**
This PR closes #743 

**What does this PR do?**
This PR removes the manifest job from test_and_deploy.yml, keeping the one in pre-commit-config.yaml, so that we can get fast feedback locally, and pre-commit.ci will also run this check remotely. This keeps the CI steps simple.

## Checklist:

- [ ] The code has been tested locally
- [ ] Tests have been added
